'''Jon Roper''' (born {{birth date|1976|05|05|df=y}}) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce|wg|so}} and {{rlp|lf}} in the 1990s and 2000s.

He played at representative level for [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Warrington Wolves]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Workington Town]]. He played in numerous positions including; {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|so}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==International honours==
Roper won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Warrington in 1999 against [[France national rugby league team|France]]. Roper also toured with [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in 1996.

==Genealogical information==
Jon Roper is the son of the rugby league {{rlp|wg}} who played in the 1980s for Workington Town; Tony Roper,<ref name="Tony Roper Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/anthony-roper/summary.html|title=Tony Roper Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> and the grandson of the rugby league footballer; [[Sol Roper]].<ref name="Tributes for Town legend Sol Roper">{{cite web|url=http://www.timesandstar.co.uk/sport/town/Tributes-for-Town-legend-Sol-Roper--e16e6a04-584e-47cc-a7bb-20e0c7c23057-ds|title=Tributes for Town legend Sol Roper|publisher=timesandstar.co.uk|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015}}</ref>
